mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit designed for Kubernetes, enabling users to build, deploy, and manage machine learning workflows. It is particularly useful for AI practitioners and platform administrators looking for a modular and scalable solution. Key trade-offs include the complexity of installation and management compared to simpler ML solutions.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for managing Kubeflow resources.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kubeflow CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster where Kubeflow will be deployed. Adjust the node count and region as needed.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1 --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step deploys Kubeflow to your Kubernetes cluster using the Kubeflow CLI.

```bash

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

```

> Deploy Kubeflow using the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if all the Kubeflow components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow

```

> List all pods in the kubeflow namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply monitoring configurations.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

kfctl delete -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Delete the Kubeflow deployment.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade your Kubeflow installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kubeflow > kubeflow-backup.txt

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the official installation guide.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubeflow CLI is not installed or not correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Kubeflow CLI and ensure it's in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'kubeflow' namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more components are failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the failing pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeflow'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your GKE cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new ML frameworks, improved integration with Kubernetes 1.30.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy APIs for model serving.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to update your configurations to align with the new API specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see all Kubeflow components running with 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow' showing 'Running' status for all pods.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a GKE account and the necessary permissions to create clusters.
